qas.q: Risk Management Framework (RMF) is the unified information security framework for the entire federal government that is replacing the legacy DIACAP (DoD Information Assurance Certification and Accreditation Process) processes within federal government departments and agencies, the Department of Defense (DOD) and the Intelligence Community (IC).
DIACAP is the certification and accreditation (C&A) process that was implemented in 2006 replacing DITSCAP. DIACAP has now been replaced by the RMF process.

Step 1 - CATEGORIZE System
Step 2 - SELECT Security Controls
Step 3 - IMPLEMENT Security Controls
Step 4 - ASSESS Security Controls
Step 5 - AUTHORIZE Systems
Step 6 - MONITOR Security Controls
qas.a: What are the steps in the RMF process?
_________________ is the high level document dated march 2014 that sets forth policy stating that RMF is to be used by DoD.
_________________ is the guide for applying RMF to Federal Information Systems.

Implementation of security controls specified in the security plan will be in accordance with DoD implementation guidance for each security control found on the _____________ _____________ _____________ page of the RMF Knowledge Service site.

qas.q:
- high level document that sets forth the policy
- go-to source when working with RMF
- None of the above
qas.a:
NIST Special Publication 800-37 is the:

- high level document that sets forth the policy
- guide for applying RMF to federal information systems
- go-to source when working with RMF
- None of the above
Security controls are implemented consistent with DoD and DoD Component IA architectures and standards, security engineering principles, and secure coding techniques. (True or False)

To increase overall efficiency and cost effectiveness of security control implementation:

- use automated support tools
- maximize communications
- reference existing documentation
All of the above
If you already use the eMASS to document security control implementation you should continue to do so. (True or False)

The THIRD step in the RMF process is to:

- Categorize the system
- approve security plan
- Monitor security control
- implement security controls

Who must translate security control into system specifications, and ensure the successful integrations into the system design?

- Program Manager
- Mission Owner
- Information Owner
- Information System Owner
It isn't important to address the proposed system security design in preliminary and critical design reviews. (True or False)

Program Managers for programs acquiring information or platform IT systems in accordance with DoD Instruction 5000.02 must integrate the security engineering of cybersecurity requirements and cybersecurity testing considerations into the program's overall systems engineering process. (True or False)

Who has primary responsibility for the two tasks that comprise step 3 of the RMF?

- Information System Owner
- Information Owner
- Mission Owner
- Program Manager

Implementation Guidance provided in Security Controls Explorer covers specific control documentation requirements, including:

- templates
- required artifacts
- best practices
- all of the above

Who has the chief responsibility for the two tasks that comprise step 3 of the RMF?

- Information Owner
- ISSO
- Information System Owner
- Mission Owner
